title: EP-RECOURSE-REFERENCE-v1
description: A signed, offline-verifiable reference to WHO stands behind a specific authorized agent action, and under what terms. EMILIA proves the reference — that a named responsible party committed to these terms and bound the commitment to a GENUINE authorization for this EXACT action. EMILIA does NOT bear the loss, adjudicate coverage, determine solvency, or make any legal determination: it is claim-not-guarantee, evidence-not-adjudication. It composes above the authorization receipt (draft-schrock-ep-authorization-receipts) by digest, and is the socket an insurer / surety / employer / facilitator plugs into. Extends the named-owner liability block in lib/provenance/chain.js.

subject_digest.description: SHA-256 over JCS(action) — the exact action this recourse is FOR. Same join key as the authorization receipt and the capsule seam.

authorization.description: The authorization this recourse rides. receipt_payload_digest binds the reference to a GENUINE approval — recourse cannot be attached to a bare claim or a forged authorization.

responsible_party.description: WHO is on the hook. EMILIA proves this party SIGNED the commitment; it does not verify solvency or enforce payment.

entity.description: stable id (did:web / urn / issuer entity id) of the responsible legal entity

role.description: self = the operator stands behind their own agent; employer = the deploying org; third_party = an external guarantor/insurer/surety.

exclusions_digest.description: SHA-256 of the canonical exclusions document. EMILIA binds the exclusions by hash; it does NOT interpret them or decide whether a loss is covered — that adjudication is the responsible party's.

dispute_endpoint.description: where a claimant files (opaque to EMILIA)

settlement_instruction.description: opaque reference to how settlement occurs (EMILIA never moves funds)

evidence_requirements.description: the evidence artifacts a claim must present (e.g. EP-RECEIPT-v1, EP-EXECUTION-ATTESTATION-v1) — the reason EMILIA receipts make the action underwritable at all.

status_url.description: revocation/status endpoint. Offline verification proves the SIGNED terms; whether the recourse is still LIVE is server-state, checked here (same authenticity-vs-currency split as receipt revocation).

issuer_key.description: base64url SPKI DER of the responsible party's signing key. ACCEPTANCE requires the relying party (gateway/claimant) to have PINNED this issuer out-of-band — a self-asserted recourse reference VERIFIES but is never ACCEPTED unpinned (same verified-vs-accepted discipline as federation).
